announcementBar.message: Supporter v1 is live for prepaid credits, visible ledger history, and EarnRM redemption. Swap remains preview-only.

footer.description: Prepaid ecosystem utility credits with one visible balance, clear activity history, and supported product usage rights.

wallet.title: UNYT Wallet
wallet.description: Supporter-facing wallet view with account setup, sign-in, backend funding flow, recorded allocation, activity history, and supported redemption.

activity.title: Activity
activity.description: Unified credit activity history for supporter v1 wallet actions and redemptions.
activity.ledgerBody: Allocation, top-up, and redemption events are recorded in this activity feed.

swap.title: Swap roadmap preview
swap.description: Swap execution is not live in supporter v1. The live supporter surface is the UNYT wallet with balance, activity history, and redemption.
swap.heroBody: Supporter v1 is intentionally wallet-first: prepaid credits, recorded balance visibility, activity history, and redeemable usage rights. Swap remains preview-only until production routing, compliance checks, and settlement handling are ready.
swap.liveNowBody: Top-up, balance visibility, activity history, and EarnRM redemption.
swap.previewBody: Pair quotes, route selection, and settlement confirmation remain roadmap items.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Supporter account profile, session controls, and backend-generated follow-up operations.

admin.title: Superadmin
admin.description: View all supporter wallets and edit account profile, allocation, and non-crypto balance records.

hero.subheadline: Prepaid ecosystem credits with clear balance visibility and real supporter utility.
hero.body: UNYT.shop v1 is built for practical use: buy prepaid credits, hold a visible account balance, review activity history, and redeem into supported product usage rights. Swap is intentionally held in preview until production execution rails are ready.
hero.note: Card top-up is currently a supporter-ready scaffold. Production settlement and webhook confirmation are the next integration phase.

redemptionFeature.title: Redeem credits into supported product usage rights.
redemptionFeature.body: Supporter v1 keeps the first path practical: top up prepaid credits, then redeem into supported rights. EarnRM user-month credit is live now, and additional routes are clearly marked as planned.

bullets.1: Every redemption writes to activity history
bullets.2: Live and planned product status shown directly

faqSection.title: Answers before users need support.
faqSection.body: Keep practical questions close to the conversion path with direct answers and clear availability language.
faqSection.promiseTitle: Clear status, practical answers, and support paths that feel operational.
faqSection.promiseBody: UNYT.shop should explain what is live and what is planned before supporters need to ask, then keep resolution routes visible when they do.

0.title: A wallet experience supporters can understand quickly.
0.body: UNYT.shop v1 is built around prepaid ecosystem utility credits, not abstract token claims. Top up, hold a visible balance, and redeem into supported product usage rights.

bullets.0: Prepaid credits with clear account-level visibility
bullets.1: One balance model across participating products
bullets.2: Practical scope that avoids feature overclaiming

1.title: Every action has clear status and next steps.
1.body: Supporters should always know what is live, what is preview-only, and what happens after each action. The interface keeps balance, activity, and redemption status easy to verify.

bullets.0: Live and planned features are labeled directly
bullets.1: Activity history confirms top-ups and redemptions
bullets.2: Support language is concise and operational

hero.title: Supported products with clear live and planned status.
hero.body: UNYT.shop v1 starts with a believable supporter scope: prepaid credits, visible balance, and live EarnRM redemption, with additional routes listed as planned.

supportedProducts.title: A practical product set, not a placeholder promise.
supportedProducts.body: Each card shows a real use case and should be read with live versus planned status in mind.

howSpendingWorks.title: Simple for supporters, structured for rollout.
howSpendingWorks.body: One credit model supports top-up, balance visibility, activity history, and supported redemption without pretending every route is live on day one.

0.title: Top up once
0.body: Supporters add prepaid credits through a guided top-up flow with clear amount controls.

1.title: Track every movement
1.body: The same account shows available credits, pending amounts, and ledger history in one place.

2.title: Redeem where live
2.body: Usage rights are redeemable on supported products now, while planned routes remain explicitly labeled.

partner.title: Bring your product into the ecosystem.
partner.body: For partners, the goal is straightforward: a coherent prepaid credit experience with clear availability states across brands.
partner.countBody: {count} ecosystem examples currently define the supporter narrative. New partners can extend the same balance model with explicit rollout and support states.
partner.note: Integration and settlement details are phased by readiness. Product language is kept deliberately precise so supporters are never asked to infer availability.

hero.title: A supporter-ready path from top-up to redemption.
hero.body: The model is intentionally practical: buy prepaid credits, keep a visible balance, see activity history, and redeem into supported product usage rights.

relationship.title: Why the three steps belong together.
relationship.body: Each stage lowers ambiguity. Top-up creates the balance, activity confirms the record, and redemption converts credits into supported usage rights.

0.title: Buy prepaid credits
0.body: Top up with supported payment methods and add UNYTs to your account balance.

1.title: Track balance and activity
1.body: See available credits, pending amounts, and a clear ledger of each top-up and redemption.

2.title: Redeem supported usage rights
2.body: Use credits for live product rights, starting with EarnRM user-month access, while additional products roll out in planned phases.

network.title: One model, rolled out across participating products.
network.body: Supporters should see where credits are usable today and where routes remain planned.

hero.title: Straight answers to practical supporter questions.
hero.body: The product should explain what UNYTs are, what is live now, and what remains preview before supporters need to contact support.

callout.title: Clarity first, then conversion.
callout.body: Keep explanations concise, direct, and commercially grounded. This is a prepaid utility credit experience, not speculative product positioning.

hero.title: Built for control, visibility, and mature support.
hero.body: Security language stays clear and measured. Supporters should understand what is protected, what they can verify, and where support begins when something needs attention.

principles.title: A serious product surface starts with product discipline.
principles.body: Good security communication avoids exaggeration. It gives users clear expectations, useful controls, and visible status.

walletSafety.title: Clear guidance around account, payments, and recovery.
walletSafety.body: The interface should help users understand sensitive actions without forcing them to decipher technical detail.

supportDisclosure.title: Availability depends on region, account status, and supported routes.
supportDisclosure.body: This v1 intentionally avoids claims beyond current support. Users should see availability, confirmation states, and support next steps before action.
supportDisclosure.riskBody: Wallet features, payment routes, and future swap availability may vary by region, account requirements, and supported infrastructure. The product should say so directly.
supportDisclosure.recoveryBody: Support should verify identity where required, communicate timing clearly, and avoid making guarantees the system cannot keep.

hero.title: UNYTBOT
hero.subtitle: A future trading intelligence and automation layer for active ecosystem participants.
hero.body: UNYTBOT is being built as a practical decision-support and automation product direction within the UNYT ecosystem. The goal is to help users evaluate setups faster, execute repeatable workflows more clearly, and connect activity with broader UNYT account continuity.

status.title: Active product development with visible milestones.
status.body: UNYTBOT is in development and is not yet a live released trading product. Current work is focused on concept validation, prototypes, and launch readiness.
status.flowBody: Interest captured here is routed into the Fintery / EarnRM follow-up flow with structured source tagging.

what.title: Product direction: trading support that is practical, not abstract.
what.body: UNYTBOT is intended to become a working intelligence layer for users who need clearer market workflow support inside the UNYT ecosystem.
what.detail: The focus is decision support, workflow automation, and better operational usability. It is not positioned as a performance-guarantee product and does not claim live readiness today.

capabilities.0: setup monitoring and signal interpretation support
capabilities.1: automation for repeatable actions and routine workflows
capabilities.3: clearer execution support with staged usability improvements

audience.0: Active market participants who want faster structured analysis
audience.1: Ecosystem users needing repeatable workflow support and automation
audience.2: Commercial users exploring integrated UNYT-linked trading tooling

why.title: Why this direction is commercially relevant.
why.body: UNYTBOT is meant to convert ecosystem interest into a tangible product path with clearer practical utility.

bullets.0: Defines a concrete utility layer beyond placeholder messaging
bullets.1: Links intelligence support with real operational workflows
bullets.2: Creates a structured early-access path for qualified interest
bullets.3: Keeps claims disciplined: no promised performance, no premature launch framing

roadmap.title: Roadmap: phased path to prototype and launch target.
roadmap.body: The program is structured into clear product stages so progress, risk, and timing can be assessed quickly.
roadmap.caveat: Milestones are directional targets and can shift based on prototype quality, testing outcomes, and operating readiness.

bullets.0: product definition and target user profiling

lead.title: Join the UNYTBOT early-interest list
lead.body: Register now to receive prototype updates, launch-timeline updates, and early-access communication.
lead.detail: This page is part of the wider UNYT ecosystem expansion path. All submissions route into the Fintery / EarnRM follow-up flow for structured next steps.
lead.footerNote: UNYTBOT is in development and not yet a live released trading product.
lead.formBody: Register your interest to receive prototype updates, early-access news, and future release information for UNYTBOT.

form.sharedBody: If you would like to receive updates, request early access, or discuss how this could fit your interests, please leave your details below. All submissions are routed into our structured Fintery / EarnRM follow-up flow.
form.success: Interest registered. We will follow up with updates.
form.error: Unable to submit interest right now. Please try again.
form.followUpNote: Source tagging and timestamp are captured automatically for follow-up routing.

hero.title: UNYT Exchange
hero.subtitle: A future exchange layer with strategic ecosystem relevance.
hero.body: UNYT Exchange is an intended long-term infrastructure direction within the UNYT ecosystem. Work is progressing through staged market, legal, compliance, and technical tracks so feasibility and readiness are built before launch decisions.

important.title: Intended direction, not a live exchange service.
important.body: This page does not provide a public exchange service or regulated transaction environment. It exists to communicate strategic intent, roadmap progression, and careful readiness criteria.

points.0: No live exchange functionality is available today.
points.1: No claim is made that licenses or approvals are already secured.
points.2: Progress and timing depend on legal, regulatory, compliance, and operational readiness.

important.continuity: UNYT Exchange is one part of a broader UNYT ecosystem expansion path across complementary utility layers.

what.title: A future ecosystem exchange and financial usability layer.
what.body: The direction is to build a credible exchange-oriented layer that can improve long-term utility, instrument access, and ecosystem financial usability.
what.detail: The approach is intentionally staged and compliance-aware. Legal structure, jurisdiction choices, licensing preparation, and operating controls are treated as core workstreams.

focus.0: research and market review for actual demand
focus.2: legal structuring and operating model design
focus.4: licensing and approval preparation where required

why.title: Why this matters for backers and ecosystem participants.
why.body: A future exchange layer can provide clearer long-term financial usability logic across the UNYT ecosystem if built responsibly and in sequence.

bullets.0: Improves strategic clarity on where ecosystem financial utility can evolve
bullets.1: Creates a structured path for backer, partner, and commercial conversations
bullets.2: Keeps progression grounded in legal and compliance realities before launch

why.ecosystemLine: UNYT Exchange is not a standalone domain play. It is intended to sit alongside UNYT wallet and product surfaces as part of one ecosystem continuity model.

roadmap.title: Roadmap: staged work toward end-2026 launch readiness.
roadmap.body: The roadmap is structured so legal, regulatory, and operating dependencies are visible from the start.
roadmap.targetValue: Target: staged go-live toward the end of 2026
roadmap.caveat: Timing is directional and depends on legal, regulatory, compliance, and operational readiness milestones.

bullets.0: market demand analysis and segment review

bullets.1: licensing and approval preparation where required

bullets.2: timing kept conditional on readiness criteria

lead.title: Register interest in UNYT Exchange
lead.body: Register now to receive roadmap updates and future information as this direction progresses.
lead.detail: This page is part of the wider UNYT ecosystem expansion path. Interest capture routes into the Fintery / EarnRM follow-up flow for structured communication.
lead.footerNote: UNYT Exchange is an intended future direction, not a live exchange service.
lead.formBody: Register your interest to receive roadmap updates and future information as the UNYT Exchange concept progresses.

form.sharedBody: If you would like to receive updates, request early access, or discuss how this could fit your interests, please leave your details below. All submissions are routed into our structured Fintery / EarnRM follow-up flow.
form.success: Interest registered. We will follow up with updates.
form.error: Unable to submit interest right now. Please try again.
form.followUpNote: Source tagging and timestamp are captured automatically for follow-up routing.

closingCta.title: Start with credits you can actually use.
closingCta.body: Supporter v1 stays commercially credible: prepaid ecosystem credits, visible balance and activity history, plus live EarnRM redemption into supported usage rights.

howItWorksGrid.title: How UNYTs work
howItWorksGrid.body: A straightforward supporter path: top up prepaid credits, track account history, and redeem supported usage rights.

0.title: Buy prepaid credits
0.body: Top up with supported payment methods and add UNYTs to your account balance.

1.title: Track balance and activity
1.body: See available credits, pending amounts, and a clear ledger of each top-up and redemption.

2.title: Redeem supported usage rights
2.body: Use credits for live product rights, starting with EarnRM user-month access, while additional products roll out in planned phases.

heroMockup.focus: Supporter v1 focus: top-up, activity, and real redemption rights.

supporterActivity.empty: No activity has been recorded yet.

hero.unregisteredBody: Create or link a supporter account to activate wallet balance, funding actions, and redemption routes.

scope.title: Wallet support scope
scope.body: This wallet provides account-linked balance visibility, activity history, funding, and supported redemption routes. Swap remains a roadmap preview.

transferStatus.title: UNYT transfer status
transferStatus.body: The actual transfer path runs through the guided transfer page. Supporters can submit a public wallet address for assisted review and later check the reference number there.

topUp.title: Fund account
topUp.body: Top-ups are processed through backend wallet routes and immediately written to activity history.

redeem.body: Live redemption currently supports EarnRM user-month usage rights.
redeem.registrationRequired: Registration required before redemption is available.
redeem.registrationBody: Link supporter identity first, then redeem supported usage rights.

trustBand.title: Built for trust, not hype.
trustBand.body: Every part of UNYT.shop should communicate control, transparency, and product maturity: from clear balances and visible activity to direct support and disclosure.

0.title: Clear scope
0.body: The product states exactly what is live now and what remains preview-only.

1.title: Visible balance and ledger
1.body: Supporters can verify credits, pending amounts, and completed activity without ambiguity.

2.title: Operational support
2.body: Support and recovery guidance is written for practical resolution, not marketing language.

0.question: What are UNYTs?
0.answer: UNYTs are prepaid ecosystem utility credits used for supported products and services inside the UNYT network.

1.question: What is live now in supporter v1?
1.answer: Supporter v1 is live for credit top-up scaffolding, visible balance, activity history, and EarnRM redemption into usage rights.

2.question: Where can I use UNYTs today?
2.answer: Current supporter-ready redemption starts with EarnRM user-month credit. Additional product routes are listed with clear live or planned status.

3.question: How do I top up credits?
3.answer: You select an amount, choose a supported method, and confirm. The completed top-up appears in your balance and activity ledger.

4.question: Can I see my balance and activity history?
4.answer: Yes. The wallet shows available credits, pending credits, and a timestamped ledger of top-ups and redemptions.

5.question: Is swap live?
5.answer: No. Swap remains a roadmap preview in supporter v1 while execution, compliance, and settlement rails are completed.

6.question: Are UNYTs e-money or an investment product?
6.answer: No. UNYTs are positioned as utility credits for supported product usage rights, not as an investment or speculative financial instrument.

7.question: How do I get support?
7.answer: Use the wallet support path for account and redemption questions. Responses focus on practical resolution steps and clear timing expectations.

logoStrip.title: Already built for real products.
logoStrip.body: From commerce and services to community and communication, UNYTs are designed to work where people actually transact.
